note: Full SHAP TreeExplainer values available in gold360_v3/outputs/figures/ (FIG_59-62)

feature_importance.explanation: Higher delivery gaps increase risk score. Primary signal for delivery shortfall detection.
feature_importance.shap_direction: positive — higher values push predictions toward high risk

feature_importance.explanation: Deviation from median efficiency (both high and low) signals anomaly. Used in pseudo-label at 10% weight.
feature_importance.shap_direction: nonlinear — extreme values (high or low) push toward high risk

feature_importance.explanation: Higher ore processing volumes associated with lower risk (larger formal operations).
feature_importance.shap_direction: negative — higher values push toward low risk

feature_importance.explanation: ASM mines have higher risk scores. Binary flag (1=ASM, 0=formal).
feature_importance.shap_direction: positive — ASM flag pushes toward high risk

feature_importance.explanation: Higher FX spreads indicate arbitrage pressure. Used in pseudo-label at 20% weight.
feature_importance.shap_direction: positive — wider spreads push toward high risk

feature_importance.explanation: Higher inflation increases economic pressure on mining operations.
feature_importance.shap_direction: positive — higher inflation pushes toward high risk

feature_importance.explanation: Informal/Pending licenses (higher encoding) associated with higher risk.
feature_importance.shap_direction: positive — Informal(2)/Pending(3) push toward high risk

feature_importance.explanation: Closer to border = higher risk. Distance inversely related to risk.
feature_importance.shap_direction: negative — smaller distances push toward high risk

feature_importance.explanation: Sigmoid-transformed border proximity. Used in pseudo-label at 15% weight.
feature_importance.shap_direction: positive — higher border risk pushes toward high risk

feature_importance.explanation: Active policy events in the month increase risk. Used in pseudo-label at 15% weight.
feature_importance.shap_direction: positive — policy events push toward high risk

feature_importance.explanation: Gold price fluctuations affect mining economics. Non-monotonic relationship.
feature_importance.shap_direction: nonlinear — extreme prices (high or low) push toward high risk

feature_importance.explanation: 3-month rolling mean of delivery gap. Sustained gaps increase risk.
feature_importance.shap_direction: positive — sustained gaps push toward high risk

feature_importance.explanation: Longer payment delays indicate cash flow stress.
feature_importance.shap_direction: positive — longer delays push toward high risk

feature_importance.explanation: Volatility in delivery gaps. Inconsistent delivery patterns increase risk.
feature_importance.shap_direction: positive — higher volatility pushes toward high risk

feature_importance.explanation: Better FGR access reduces risk (easier formal delivery).
feature_importance.shap_direction: negative — better access pushes toward low risk

feature_importance.explanation: Extreme rainfall disrupts mining operations. Non-monotonic.
feature_importance.shap_direction: nonlinear — extreme rainfall (high or low) pushes toward high risk

feature_importance.explanation: Farther from FGR increases delivery friction.
feature_importance.shap_direction: positive — greater distance pushes toward high risk

ablation_impact.interpretation: Most critical group — delivery signals are primary risk indicators
Operational data (ore grade, rainfall, payment) provides secondary signal
Spatial features provide minor but consistent contribution
Policy and license data provides minor contribution
Macro indicators provide minimal standalone contribution
